summary: Decompiled several functions in the fighter ft_0892 unit and added supporting fighter type/header declarations. The PR fully matched ft_800892D4, ft_80089460, and fn_8008998C, added a 97.81% ft_80089B08 implementation, and improved ft_800895E0 to 98.86% according to the decomp.dev bot report. The changes centered on fighter damage/flag state initialization, hit/attack state packing, and ground IK/slope adjustment logic using newly declared IKState and x2085 bitfields.
what_changed: Implemented ft_800892D4, replacing a placeholder with initialization of Fighter damage/bonus-bit state, x2070/x2074 packed state, bitfields, knockback-related fields, and x2088.
Implemented ft_80089460, clearing x2074 vector/flag state and then deriving x2074 flags from fighter percent, metal state, x197C, and several x221D/x2226/x2220 bitflags.

Implemented ft_800895E0 as a near-match: accepts an int packed state, updates x2088 via plAttack_80037B08 when the packed subfield changes, special-cases Luigi state 0x71 to 0x240063, special-cases state 0x62 with held item/it_8026B6C8 to 0x44003D, then refreshes x2074 state.

Implemented fn_8008998C with a concrete prototype s32 fn_8008998C(Fighter*, IKState*, Vec3*); it computes an IK target vertical adjustment against the current floor using mpLib_8004DD90_Floor, mpFloorGetLeft/Right fallback points, and clamps slope contribution to +/-0.45.

Added a large mostly matching ft_80089B08 body for grounded fighter IK and body rotation: uses ft_data->x58 as a local ftData_x58_t layout, builds IKState for left/right limb paths under x221C_u16_y flags, calls lbBgFlash IK helpers, restores saved JObj rotations, and rotates fighter part 0 based on floor normal/adjacent line angle capped by p_ftCommonData->x804.
Added ftData_x58_t as a local struct in ft_0892.c to describe offsets used from fp->ft_data->x58, including joint indices and limb lengths.
Extended the x2074 union/struct in src/melee/ft/types.h with x2085_b0 through x2085_b7 bitfields.
Added struct IKState to src/melee/ft/types.h and forward-declared IKState in src/melee/ft/forward.h.
Updated ft_0892.h to replace fn_8008998C(UNK_PARAMS) with the typed Fighter*, IKState*, Vec3* prototype.
Added dependencies/includes for item, lb vector/BG flash, mpLib, MSL math/trig, and baselib JObj needed by the new implementations.
smart_moves: Kept the unknown ft_data->x58 layout local as ftData_x58_t instead of prematurely promoting a weakly understood data structure globally.
Used explicit packed-state unions and casts around x2070/x2074 fields, which matches the evidence that these fighter fields are overlayed integers/vectors/bitfields rather than simple typed members.
Documented uncertainty directly with the TODO comment on ft_800895E0: "is arg1 not just a Struct2070?", preserving a useful future investigation point instead of overclaiming the parameter type.
Handled MWCC big-endian bitfield ordering explicitly via the existing local UnkStruct89768 comment and added x2085 bitfields in the same packed fighter-state area.
In ft_80089B08, saved and restored JObj rotations around IK helper calls, avoiding accidental persistent transform mutation while still using helper routines to compute/adjust IK.

For short floor lines, sampled adjacent floor normals and blended/overrode the angle only when the difference exceeded a small threshold, reflecting careful reconstruction of edge-case slope behavior.
decomp_lessons: When fighter state fields are represented both as integer packets and named bitfields, matching code may require unions and pointer casts to force the same stores as the original compiler.
Big-endian MWCC bitfield order matters; comments and field ordering around bitfield structs are important evidence for future struct work.
Do not globally name or finalize data-file layouts too early; a local temporary struct such as ftData_x58_t can unblock matching while signaling that the layout is still provisional.

Ground IK code often depends on exact stack layout, temporary Vec3/Quaternion ordering, and helper-call side effects; preserving local variable structure and explicit rotation saves can be necessary for matching.
Near-matches can still add valuable type information: ft_800895E0 and ft_80089B08 were below 100%, but the PR exposed useful packed-state and IK structure details for later cleanup.
Use decomp.dev match reports as evidence of progress and residual work: this PR produced 4 new matches while leaving ft_80089B08 and ft_800895E0 as improvement targets.
naming_conventions: Unknown address-based functions remained address-named: ft_800892D4, ft_80089460, ft_800895E0, fn_8008998C, ft_80089B08.
The helper with no clear subsystem prefix stayed as fn_8008998C rather than being given a speculative semantic name.
Unknown struct/data layouts used placeholder names with offset hints such as ftData_x58_t, UnkStruct89768, and Struct2070.
Fighter bitfields retained offset-style names such as x2084_b1, x2085_b3, x221D_b6, and x2220_b5.
The new IKState type is semantically named because its fields and use in lbBgFlash IK helpers made the role clear enough.
Header prototypes use concrete project types where known: Fighter*, Fighter_GObj*, IKState*, Vec3*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used casts like *(s32*) &fp->x2074.x2074_vec.x = 0 and *(s32*) &fp->x2074.x2074_vec.x = (s32) fp->dmg.x1830_percent to force exact integer stores into vector/packed storage.
Used union Struct2070 temporaries on stack for packed integer assignments and subfield checks, likely to reproduce original register/stack behavior around x2070 state.
Used explicit float constants for radians and thresholds, including 0.0001f, 0.45f, 1.2217305f, 0.34906584f, 0.17453292f, and 0.017453292f * p_ftCommonData->x804.
Included MSL math_ppc/trigf and called atan2f/sqrtf rather than replacing with approximations, matching the original math library behavior.
Inserted PAD_STACK(16) at the end of ft_80089B08, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for the partial match.
Declared unused byte arrays in fn_8008998C and ft_80089B08 local stacks, suggesting local padding/stack layout was used to improve matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code increased by 1160 bytes (+0.03%) and matched data by 16 bytes; new full matches were ft_800892D4, ft_80089460, fn_8008998C, and .sdata; ft_80089B08 and ft_800895E0 remained partial at 97.81% and 98.86% respectively.
follow_up_queries: Investigate whether ft_800895E0 arg1 should be typed as union Struct2070 instead of int, as noted by the in-code TODO.
Finish matching ft_80089B08 from 97.81% to 100%, focusing on stack padding, local ordering, IKState construction, and floor-line angle adjustment.
Finish matching ft_800895E0 from 98.86% to 100%, focusing on packed Struct2070 temporaries and special-case constants 0x240063 and 0x44003D.
Validate whether local ftData_x58_t should become a shared named fighter data attribute struct once more x58 usages are understood.
Check downstream uses of the newly added x2085_b0 through x2085_b7 bitfields to improve naming beyond offset-style placeholders.
Confirm the semantics of db_804D4AF8 gating the IK path in ft_80089B08.
